bird.name: 白尾海雕
bird.summary: 白尾海雕（學名：Haliaeetus albicilla），是鷹科中一種非常大型的猛禽。很多人認為牠們是美國白頭海雕的近親，並深信牠們在舊大陸居住在同一個生態位。 外觀 白尾海雕是一種大型的鳥類，長76到100公分，翼展長200至250公分，體重介乎於3.5到7.5公斤之間。雌…

locations.name: 格陵兰
locations.summary: 格陵蘭島（格陵蘭語：Kalaallit Nunaat，發音：[kalaːɬːit nʉnaːt]；丹麥語：Grønland，發音：[ˈkʁɶnˌlænˀ]）是丹麥王國框架內之自治領地。格陵蘭島是世界最大島嶼，位於北美洲東北，介乎北冰洋與大…

locations.name: 冰岛
locations.summary: 冰岛（冰島語：Ísland，發音：[ˈistlant] ）是北欧的一个共和制岛国，位于北大西洋和北冰洋交汇处。冰岛国土面积为10.3万平方公里，人口为39万，儘管面積不大，卻是欧洲人口密度最低的国家，也是世界范围内人口密度很小的国家之一。…

locations.name: 北海道
locations.summary: 北海道（日語：北海道／ほっかいどう〔ほくかいだう〕，羅馬化：Hokkaidō，）是日本最北的一級行政區，亦是日本现时唯一以「道」為名的行政区划，首府為札幌市。北海道面積共83,423.84平方公里（包括仍有爭議的南千島群島在內），是日本面…

locations.name: 日本
locations.summary: 日本國（日語：日本国〔日本國〕／にっぽんこく／にほんこく，羅馬化：nippon koku/nihon koku），通稱日本，是位於東亞的島嶼國家。國土全境由北海道、本州、四國、九州等本土四島為基礎的14,125座島嶼組成，周圍被太平洋及鄂…

locations.name: 中亚
locations.summary: 中亚（英語：Central Asia；俄語：Средняя Азия / Центральная Азия）即亚洲中部，在地理上是指西至里海，东到中国新疆，南到阿富汗，北到俄罗斯的广大区域。而根据长期占据这片区域大部分领土的前苏联官方定义…

locations.name: 西伯利亚
locations.summary: 西伯利亞（俄語：Сибирь，羅馬化：Sibir），又名「羅荒野」、「沉睡之地」、「鮮卑利亞」、「錫伯利亞」，是烏拉山脈以東廣大地區之總稱，佔北亚的大部分，面積超过1310万平方千米，東至太平洋、北至北冰洋、南至中亚北部。自16世纪下半叶…

locations.name: 島嶼
locations.summary: 島嶼或島（英語：island）是指於海洋、江河或湖泊中被水体圍繞，面积小于大陆的陆地区域。世界上面积最大的岛屿是北美洲的格陵兰岛，面积更大的陸地則不再被視為島嶼，而叫做「大陸」。在狹小的地域集中2個或以上的島嶼，即成「島嶼群」，大規模的島…

relations.subject: 白尾海雕
relations.evidence: Off. While found across a wide range, today breeding from as far west as Greenland and Iceland across to as far east as Hokkaido, Japan, they are often scarce and spottily distributed as a nesting species, mainly due to human activities

relations.subject: 白尾海雕
relations.evidence: thumb|right|White-tailed eagles are fairly associated with wooded areas but usually ones quite near water. White-tailed eagles may be found in varied habitat but usually are closely associated with water and generally occurs in lowland areas. Although mainly a lowland species, the species is known to live at elevations of so long as there is water access in some parts of Central Asia and Siberia. In coastal areas, the species may range from high sea cliffs down to low-lying islands and archipelagos

relations.subject: 白尾海雕
relations.evidence: These have included habitat alterations and destruction of wetlands, about a hundred years of systematic persecution by humans (from the early 1800s to around World War II) followed by inadvertent poisonings and epidemics of nesting failures due to various manmade chemical pesticides and organic compounds, which have threatened eagles since roughly the 1950s and continue to be a potential concern
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: 白尾海雕
relations.evidence: Journal of Ornithology, 157(1), 311–323. In many areas, white-tailed eagles can seem to switch freely between usually cliff habitat and wooded spots for nesting sites and the center of their home range habitat.Evans, R
relations.object_summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。

relations.subject: 白尾海雕
relations.evidence: Population size, distribution and habitat selection of the white-tailed eagle Haliaeetus albicilla in the alluvial wetlands of Croatia
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: 白尾海雕
relations.evidence: Forest Ecology and Management, 185(3), 217–223. On the other hand, from studying wintering white-tailed eagles in partially or heavily disturbed wetlands in parts of the Netherlands shows that such areas cannot support the eagles for any long-term period and may only be visited for a day or two by individual eagles. Conversely, reviews of the rapidly increasing Danish population have found that when well-protected, they may regularly forage in habitats near significant humans populations, even in the outskirts of cities, and they can also successfully breed in such locations
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: 白尾海雕
relations.evidence: These have included habitat alterations and destruction of wetlands, about a hundred years of systematic persecution by humans (from the early 1800s to around World War II) followed by inadvertent poisonings and epidemics of nesting failures due to various manmade chemical pesticides and organic compounds, which have threatened eagles since roughly the 1950s and continue to be a potential concern
relations.object_summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

relations.subject: 白尾海雕
relations.evidence: Smithsonian Inst Press. Some populations have since recovered well, due to governmental protections, dedicated conservationists and naturalists protecting habitats and nesting sites, partially regulating poaching and pesticide usage, as well as careful reintroductions into parts of their former range.{{cite book |last1=Ferguson-Lees |first1=James |last2=Christie |first2=David A
relations.object_summary: 直接捕杀或非法贸易导致种群下降。

relations.subject: 白尾海雕
relations.evidence: Biological Conservation, 4(5), 373–377. It was estimated that pesticides and metal contaminations reduced the white-tailed eagle population in Hungary from 1957 to 1967 by about 50–60%. Lead poisoning, caused by lead bullets left in carcasses that the white-tailed eagles will eat in winter, is also another issue faced by the species
relations.object_summary: 食腐鸟类因摄入铅弹残留而中毒。
